rationale: Suppresses p53 stability and transcriptional activity through ubiquitin-mediated control.
Blunts p53 transactivation and cooperates with MDM2 in dampening p53 function.
Removes activating acetylation marks from p53 and weakens transcriptional recovery.
Acetyltransferase activity can restore transcriptional competence to p53-responsive programs.
Chromatin and p53 deacetylation can suppress mutant p53 reactivation programs.
Supports p53-dependent transcription through acetylation and co-activation functions.
HIPK2 promotes activating p53 phosphorylation and apoptotic gene expression.
TIP60/KAT5-mediated acetylation is linked to p53 activation and apoptotic recovery.
Can stabilize the MDM2 axis and indirectly restrain p53 reactivation.
DNA-damage signaling can reactivate p53 pathway output through phosphorylation and checkpoint control.
CHEK2 is a canonical upstream activator of p53 signaling after genotoxic stress.
Checkpoint kinase signaling can reinforce p53 activation states under replicative stress.
Checkpoint signaling intersects with p53 stress response control.
DNA-PK participates in DNA damage signaling linked to p53 regulation.
